description: A structure rests on 'motha', the supporting part through which its load reaches the ground. It lies beneath the occupied floor and may be hidden. The word can extend to an established practical basis, but 'remotha' (reason), evidence, and a philosophical premise make different claims.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced nasal 'm' and open into stressed rounded mid-back 'o'. Place the tongue tip lightly against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th', let the air continue, and release into open 'a'.
usage_notes: An abstract use works best when the supported project or practice is clear. A foundation can hold up a bad plan as readily as a good one.

semantic_domains.creation: the supporting base established before a structure rises
semantic_domains.physical: the lowest load-carrying part of a built structure
